title: The One Where Phoebe Hates PBS

jokes.text: Since when do you take naps in that position?
jokes.setup: Monica is caught in a compromising position, claims she was napping
jokes.punchline: Rachel's incredulous response implies the position was clearly sexual
jokes.explanation: Clean setup/punchline structure with efficient delivery. The humor comes from the obvious implication without being crude.

jokes.text: That cute waiter guy that looks like a nonthreatening Ray Liotta?
jokes.setup: Asking about Monica's new guy from work
jokes.punchline: Oddly specific description combining 'cute' with 'nonthreatening Ray Liotta'
jokes.explanation: Very original and specific joke that creates a vivid mental image. The contradiction between 'nonthreatening' and Ray Liotta is perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: I had to talk loud because the movie was loud!
jokes.setup: Ross got thrown out of a movie theater for being loud
jokes.punchline: His defense shows complete lack of movie theater etiquette awareness
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ross logic - completely missing social norms while being earnest about it. Strong character integration.

jokes.text: I don't care if I said some other girl's name, you prissy old twit!
jokes.setup: Ross is trying to reach Emily through her family
jokes.punchline: He's clearly alienating the very people he needs help from
jokes.explanation: Shows Ross's desperation and social obliviousness. The insult choice is character-appropriate - nerdy but harsh.

jokes.text: Way to suck up to the family!
jokes.setup: Ross just insulted Emily's family member
jokes.punchline: Joey's sarcastic observation about Ross's terrible strategy
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective sarcasm that highlights the obvious irony of Ross's approach.

jokes.text: I'm so glad you're all here. My office finally got wrinkle-free fax paper!
jokes.setup: Chandler arrives excited to share news
jokes.punchline: His excitement is about the most mundane office upgrade possible
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chandler - finding genuine excitement in boring office minutiae. High character integration.

jokes.text: Oh, it's '4-3,' not 4-2. Well, which 4-2?
jokes.setup: Ross is trying to dial the correct international number
jokes.punchline: His confused response shows he's completely lost in the number sequence
jokes.explanation: Shows Ross's frazzled state through his inability to follow simple directions. Mildly amusing but not exceptional.

jokes.text: Vomit tux? Who vomited on?
jokes.setup: Chandler mentions he had his tux dry-cleaned
jokes.punchline: Monica's horrified reaction to learning about the vomit incident
jokes.explanation: Simple callback humor that relies on past events. Functional but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: That's the kind of math Joey likes to do.
jokes.setup: Joey explains his PBS gig will be good exposure plus a good deed
jokes.punchline: Chandler notes Joey's self-serving calculation disguised as altruism
jokes.explanation: Economical and sharp observation about Joey's character. Great economy of language with perfect character insight.

jokes.text: A lot of those muppets don't have thumbs.
jokes.setup: Phoebe complains no one from Sesame Street wrote her back
jokes.punchline: Ross offers the most literal and absurd excuse possible
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd logic delivered deadpan. The technical accuracy makes it even funnier. Highly quotable and original.

jokes.text: All I got was a lousy key chain, and by that time, I was living in a box. I didn't have keys!
jokes.setup: Phoebe describes her disappointment with PBS's response
jokes.punchline: The irony of receiving a keychain while homeless and keyless
jokes.explanation: Dark humor delivered with perfect escalation. The cruel irony is both funny and sad, classic Phoebe.

jokes.text: Are you calling me selfish? Are you calling you people?
jokes.setup: Joey asks if she's calling him selfish
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's confused grammar creates an unexpected question
jokes.explanation: Typical Phoebe verbal confusion. The grammar mistake creates an unintended philosophical question.

jokes.text: You know the deal on Santa Claus?
jokes.setup: Joey is making a point about selfless good deeds
jokes.punchline: He casually destroys childhood innocence as evidence
jokes.explanation: Joey's casual cruelty disguised as philosophical discourse. The understatement makes it funnier.

jokes.text: When you said 'the deal with Santa,' you meant...? That he doesn't exist. Right.
jokes.setup: Ross seeks clarification on Joey's Santa comment
jokes.punchline: The matter-of-fact confirmation and Ross's resigned acceptance
jokes.explanation: Simple callback that extends the Santa joke. Functional but not particularly clever on its own.

jokes.text: Really?
jokes.setup: Monica mentions her new boyfriend is the best sex she's ever had
jokes.punchline: Chandler's shocked reaction (implied awkward silence)
jokes.explanation: Simple reaction beat. The humor comes from Chandler's uncomfortable position hearing about Monica's sex life.

jokes.text: Why is that funny? Because I'm very happy for him. And you! You lucky dog!
jokes.setup: Chandler is asked why he's laughing about Monica's sex life
jokes.punchline: His over-enthusiastic attempt to seem supportive makes it worse
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Chandler's discomfort. His attempt to recover makes the situation more awkward, which is the joke.

jokes.text: But you live here. You know that.
jokes.setup: Emily wants Ross to move to London
jokes.punchline: Chandler states the obvious with deadpan delivery
jokes.explanation: Classic Chandler - pointing out the obvious with perfect deadpan timing. Simple but effective.

jokes.text: You can just video-conference with him.
jokes.setup: Ross says he can't leave Ben
jokes.punchline: Joey suggests treating parenting like a business meeting
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey logic - applying inappropriate business solutions to personal problems. Good character consistency.

jokes.text: I'm sure your ex-wife would be more than happy to move so you can patch things up with your new wife.
jokes.setup: Ross thinks he can convince Carol and Susan to move to London
jokes.punchline: Chandler sarcastically points out how ridiculous this expectation is
jokes.explanation: Good sarcastic delivery that highlights the absurdity of Ross's plan. Clear structure and good character voice.

jokes.text: Tea. They have a queen.
jokes.setup: Ross is listing reasons why London would be great
jokes.punchline: His reasons get increasingly desperate and basic
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Ross grasping at straws. The simplicity of 'tea' and 'queen' shows his desperation.

jokes.text: So? She's a woman.
jokes.setup: Carol questions why having a queen matters
jokes.punchline: Ross's non-sequitur response that makes no logical sense
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly illogical Ross response. The complete disconnect between question and answer is perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: Nobody's going to London. Welcome to London, governor.
jokes.setup: Carol firmly states no one is moving
jokes.punchline: Ben immediately contradicts her with British accent
jokes.explanation: Good timing and irony. Ben's enthusiasm undermines Carol's authority in a child-like way.

jokes.text: It'll be great because we'll have Big Ben and little Ben in the same city.
jokes.setup: Ross is still trying to sell the London move
jokes.punchline: He makes a corny pun connecting his son to the famous clock tower
jokes.explanation: Typical dad joke from Ross. The pun is obvious but fits his character perfectly. Charming but not sophisticated.

jokes.text: Yeah, we leave all our big decisions to the 3-year-old.
jokes.setup: Ross points to Ben's enthusiasm as evidence they should move
jokes.punchline: Susan sarcastically highlights the absurdity of this logic
jokes.explanation: Straightforward sarcasm that points out Ross's flawed reasoning. Functional but not particularly creative.

jokes.text: It'll be just like Three's Company, only there's a kid and my wife and you're the gay ones.
jokes.setup: Ross continues trying to convince Carol and Susan
jokes.punchline: He makes an inappropriate TV show comparison with awkward specificity
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully inappropriate Ross comparison. The specificity of the TV show analogy and awkward delivery makes it funny.

jokes.text: You don't seem to understand. I was Dr. Drake Ramoray.
jokes.setup: PBS coordinator assigns Joey to phone duty instead of hosting
jokes.punchline: Joey's indignant response using his soap opera credentials
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey ego and self-importance. His soap opera past is meaningless here, but he thinks it should matter.

jokes.text: Then I felt wonderful. That old jackass!
jokes.setup: Phoebe did a good deed but the neighbor was nice to her in return
jokes.punchline: She's angry at him for making her feel good about helping
jokes.explanation: Great Phoebe logic - being mad at someone for being kind. The anger at unexpected kindness is perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: I gave birth to three children and I won't let them be raised in a world where Joey's right!
jokes.setup: Phoebe is determined to find a selfless good deed
jokes.punchline: She frames this as a matter of universal importance and child welfare
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation that makes Joey's philosophical point into a matter of cosmic importance. Great character voice.

jokes.text: If you're cooking on the stove, that means your new boyfriend is better in bed than Richard?
jokes.setup: Chandler notices Monica cooking differently
jokes.punchline: He connects cooking method to sexual performance inappropriately
jokes.explanation: Callback to established Monica character trait. The inappropriate connection makes it uncomfortably funny.

jokes.text: If this guy was me, and it's me who'd learned that it was me who was the best you'd ever had, I'd be like this:
jokes.setup: Chandler explains how he would react to being called the best
jokes.punchline: Presumably shows an exaggerated celebration dance/pose
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy moment. Chandler's ego and awkwardness combine for a visual gag. Grammar confusion adds to the joke.

jokes.text: We're going to be like two idiots in love!
jokes.setup: Ross is excited Emily agreed to come to New York
jokes.punchline: His romantic declaration accidentally calls them both idiots
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ross moment - accidentally undermining his own romantic gesture. The self-deprecation is unintentional.

jokes.text: Thanks for the help. Problem solved!
jokes.setup: Friends give contradictory advice about Rachel vs Emily
jokes.punchline: Ross sarcastically thanks them for making the decision harder
jokes.explanation: Standard sarcastic response to unhelpful friends. Gets the job done but isn't particularly creative.

jokes.text: Maybe just put the whole duck in there. Who cares?
jokes.setup: Monica is watching cooking shows
jokes.punchline: The chef on TV gives terrible, lazy cooking advice
jokes.explanation: Good contrast with Monica's perfectionism. The casual 'who cares' attitude would horrify chef Monica.

jokes.text: How many times have I told you to never watch the cooking channel?
jokes.setup: Monica is frustrated by bad cooking advice on TV
jokes.punchline: Chandler reveals this is a recurring problem
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Chandler knows Monica's cooking obsession well enough to have rules about it.

jokes.text: We used it when the duck was throwing up caterpillars.
jokes.setup: Monica asks about her broiling pan
jokes.punchline: Chandler reveals the disgusting reason they needed it
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully gross and specific detail. The casual delivery of this disgusting image makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Williams-Sonoma, fall catalog, page 27. Expect it in 4 to 6 weeks.
jokes.setup: Monica wants her expensive broiling pan replaced
jokes.punchline: Chandler knows exactly where to order it, showing he's learned her preferences
jokes.explanation: Shows Chandler has learned to navigate Monica's expensive tastes. The specificity suggests past experience.

jokes.text: Well, I think I'd be a little out of my league, but I'd give it a shot.
jokes.setup: Monica asks if he'd want to sleep with himself given his gloating
jokes.punchline: Chandler's self-deprecating response about being out of his own league
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chandler self-deprecation. The logic of being out of his own league is absurd but delivered with confidence.

jokes.text: I went down to the park and I let a bee sting me.
jokes.setup: Phoebe claims she found a selfless good deed
jokes.punchline: She deliberately got stung by a bee
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd Phoebe logic. The idea of helping a bee by letting it sting you is wonderfully illogical.

jokes.text: It helps the bee look tough in front of his bee friends.
jokes.setup: Joey questions what good the bee sting does
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's explanation anthropomorphizes bees in ridiculous way
jokes.explanation: Exceptional absurdist humor. The idea of bee social dynamics and peer pressure is brilliantly ridiculous and perfectly delivered.

jokes.text: You know, the bee probably died after he stung you.
jokes.setup: Phoebe thinks she helped the bee
jokes.punchline: Joey points out the bee likely died from stinging her
jokes.explanation: Excellent subversion of Phoebe's logic. Joey accidentally ruins her good deed with scientific fact.

jokes.text: Damn it!
jokes.setup: Phoebe realizes she killed the bee instead of helping it
jokes.punchline: Her frustrated reaction to another failed attempt
jokes.explanation: Simple reaction that completes the bee sequence. The frustration is relatable and well-timed.

jokes.text: Quick broiling pan question
jokes.setup: Joey is on live TV taking pledges
jokes.punchline: Monica interrupts the broadcast with a cooking question
jokes.explanation: Good callback that shows Monica's single-minded focus on cooking, even during Joey's TV moment.

jokes.text: Maybe next time your duck can puke in something from Pottery Barn.
jokes.setup: Monica is angry about her expensive pan being ruined
jokes.punchline: She suggests a cheaper store for future duck vomit incidents
jokes.explanation: Great escalation combining Monica's brand consciousness with the absurd duck situation. Good character voice.

jokes.text: It's not a toy.
jokes.setup: Chandler criticizes using a Magic 8 Ball for important decisions
jokes.punchline: Ross defends it with complete seriousness
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ross moment - treating something childish with academic seriousness. Economy of language makes it effective.

jokes.text: Let me see. Will Chandler have sex tonight?
jokes.setup: Ross claims the Magic 8 Ball is broken
jokes.punchline: Chandler tests it with a question about himself
jokes.explanation: Good setup for the punchline that follows. Chandler's willingness to test his own prospects shows confidence.

jokes.text: 'Don't Count On It.' Seems like it works to me.
jokes.setup: Chandler asks if he'll have sex tonight
jokes.punchline: The ball gives a negative answer, which he accepts as accurate
jokes.explanation: Excellent self-deprecating humor. Chandler's acceptance of his romantic prospects being poor is perfectly delivered.

jokes.text: Looks like Stella's gonna get her groove back tonight!
jokes.setup: Rachel wins the Magic 8 Ball movie choice
jokes.punchline: She enthusiastically references the romantic movie they'll see
jokes.explanation: Simple pop culture reference. Gets a mild laugh but relies on external reference rather than original humor.

jokes.text: I'm still mad at them, but they bring happiness to lots of kids whose moms didn't kill themselves.
jokes.setup: Phoebe explains why she's donating to PBS despite her grudge
jokes.punchline: She matter-of-factly contrasts her tragic backstory with normal kids
jokes.explanation: Exceptional dark humor delivered with Phoebe's characteristic matter-of-fact tone. Balances tragedy with altruism perfectly.

jokes.text: A hamster? Those things are, like, $10. Not the one I had my eye on.
jokes.setup: Phoebe was saving money for a hamster
jokes.punchline: She implies she wanted an expensive hamster
jokes.explanation: Perfect Phoebe quirkiness. The idea of an expensive hamster she had her eye on is wonderfully absurd.

jokes.text: Oh, no!
jokes.setup: Phoebe's donation gets Joey on TV, making her feel good
jokes.punchline: She realizes she failed again at finding a selfless deed
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to the running gag. Phoebe's realization that she feels good ruins her selfless deed attempt.

jokes.text: If I'm the best, it's only because you made me the best.
jokes.setup: Chandler needs to make up with Monica after gloating
jokes.punchline: He gives her credit for his sexual prowess
jokes.explanation: Sweet romantic gesture that works as comedy through its earnestness. Good character moment for Chandler.

jokes.text: Call the other girls and ask. Which wouldn't take long.
jokes.setup: Chandler says he was nothing before Monica
jokes.punchline: He jokes about his limited sexual experience
jokes.explanation: Good self-deprecating humor that reinforces Chandler's character while being romantic. Efficient delivery.

jokes.text: We owe it to sex!
jokes.setup: Chandler argues they're too good in bed to stop
jokes.punchline: He frames it as a duty to sexuality itself
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation that makes their relationship cosmic in importance. The grandiosity is perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: No, no, no! Leave the gloves on.
jokes.setup: Chandler and Monica are getting romantic
jokes.punchline: Monica wants to keep rubber cleaning gloves on
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Monica's cleaning obsession extends to intimate moments. Appropriately uncomfortable.

tmdb_overview: Joey works a PBS telethon triggering Phoebe's old grudge, Monica hides her relationship with Chandler, and Ross reconnects with Emily.
